list.title: Nustatytos savybės

integration.example-conceptMap: First you need import CodeSystem resources used for concept map
integration.example-valueSet: First you need import CodeSystem resources used for value set

find-matches.exact-tooltip: Whether the operation is being used by a human ('false'), or a machine ('true'). If the operation is being used by a human, the terminology server can return a list of complete or partial matches. For a machine, the server returns complete matches. The default value is 'false'
find-matches.properties-tooltip: One or more properties that contain information to be composed into the code
find-matches.system-tooltip: The system in which composition is to be performed. This must be provided unless the operation is invoked on a code system instance
find-matches.version-tooltip: The version of the system for the inferencing to be performed

lookup.code-tooltip: The code that is to be located. If a code is provided, a system must be provided
lookup.date-tooltip:
The date for which the information should be returned. Normally, this is the current conditions (which is the default value) but under some circumstances,
systems need to acccess this information as it would have been in the past.
A typical example of this would be where code selection is constrained to the set of codes that were available when the patient was treated,
not when the record is being edited. Note that which date is appropriate is a matter for implementation policy.
lookup.properties-tooltip:
A property that the client wishes to be returned in the output. If no properties are specified, the server chooses what to return.
The following properties are defined for all code systems: url, name, version (code system info) and code information:
display, definition, designation, parent and child, and for designations, lang.X where X is a designation language code.
Some of the properties are returned explicit in named parameters (when the names match), and the rest (except for lang.X) in the property parameter group
lookup.system-tooltip: The system for the code that is to be located
lookup.version-tooltip: The version of the system, if one was provided in the source data

subsumes.code-a-tooltip: The 'A' code that is to be tested. If a code is provided, a system must be provided
subsumes.code-b-tooltip: The 'B' code that is to be tested. If a code is provided, a system must be provided
subsumes.system-tooltip: The code system in which subsumption testing is to be performed. This must be provided unless the operation is invoked on a code system instance
subsumes.version-tooltip: The version of the code system, if one was provided in the source data

validate-code.code-tooltip: The code that is to be validated
validate-code.display-tooltip:
The display associated with the code, if provided. If a display is provided a code must be provided.
If no display is provided, the server cannot validate the display value, but may choose to return a recommended display name in an extension in the outcome.
Whether displays are case sensitive is code system dependent
validate-code.url-tooltip: CodeSystem URL. The server must know the code system (e.g. it is defined explicitly in the servers code systems, or it is known implicitly by theserver)
validate-code.version-tooltip: The version of the code system, if one was provided in the source data

closure.concepts-tooltip: Concepts to add to the closure table
closure.name: Pavadinimas
closure.name-tooltip: The name that defines the particular context for the subsumption based closure table
closure.version-tooltip: A request to resynchronise - request to send all new entries since the nominated version was sent by the server

translate.code-tooltip: The code that is to be translated. If a code is provided, a system must be provided
translate.concept-map-version-tooltip:
The identifier that is used to identify a specific version of the concept map to be used for the translation.
This is an arbitrary value managed by the concept map author and is not expected to be globally unique.
For example, it might be a timestamp (e.g. yyyymmdd) if a managed version is not available.
translate.system-tooltip: The system for the code that is to be translated
translate.target-system-tooltip:
identifies a target code system in which a mapping is sought. This parameter is an alternative to the target parameter - only one is required.
Searching for any translation to a target code system irrespective of the context (e.g. target valueset) may lead to unsafe results,
and it is at the discretion of the server to decide when to support this
translate.uri-tooltip:
A canonical URL for a concept map. The server must know the concept map (e.g. it is defined explicitly in the server's concept maps,
or it is defined implicitly by some code system known to the server.
translate.version-tooltip: The version of the system, if one was provided in the source data

expand.url-tooltip: A canonical reference to a value set. The server must know the value set (e.g. it is defined explicitly in the server's value sets, or it is defined implicitly by some code system known to the server
expand.value-set-version-tooltip: The identifier that is used to identify a specific version of the value set to be used when generating the expansion. This is an arbitrary value managed by the value set author and is not expected to be globally unique. For example, it might be a timestamp (e.g. yyyymmdd) if a managed version is not available.

validate-code.code-tooltip: The code that is to be validated. If a code is provided, a system or a context must be provided (if a context is provided, then the server SHALL ensure that the code is not ambiguous without a system)
validate-code.display-tooltip: The display associated with the code, if provided. If a display is provided a code must be provided. If no display is provided, the server cannot validate the display value, but may choose to return a recommended display name using the display parameter in the outcome. Whether displays are case sensitive is code system dependent
validate-code.system-tooltip: The system for the code that is to be validated
validate-code.system-version-tooltip: The version of the system, if one was provided in the source data
validate-code.url-tooltip: Value set Canonical URL. The server must know the value set (e.g. it is defined explicitly in the server's value sets, or it is defined implicitly by some code system known to the server
validate-code.value-set-version-tooltip: The identifier that is used to identify a specific version of the value set to be used when validating the code. This is an arbitrary value managed by the value set author and is not expected to be globally unique. For example, it might be a timestamp (e.g. yyyymmdd) if a managed version is not available.

import-modal.dry-run-warning: Bandomasis paleidimas iš RF2 zip failo sukuria pakeitimų ataskaitą neįkeliant į Snowstorm. Kai ataskaita paruošta, rezultatų ekrane naudokite mygtuką "Tęsti importą", kad įkeltumėte tą patį failą.

management.import-succeeded: Importavimas iš RF2 zip archyvo sėkmingas
management.import-warning: Importavimo metu bus sukurta redagavimui skirta nomenklatūros atšaka. Kodų sistema sukurta nebus. Priklausomai nuo naudojamo SNOMED paketo dydžio, įkėlimas gali užtrukti virš 30-60 min.

form.import-warning: Importavimo metu bus sukurta atšaka ir ją atitinkanti kodų sistemos versija. Ši funkcija turi būti naudojama importuojant paskelbtą nomenklatūros versiją arba kuriant galutinę versiją iš redaguojamos atšakos. Priklausomai nuo naudojamo SNOMED paketo, įkėlimas gali užtrukti virš 30-60 min.
